title: selinux preventing loading shared library from nfs mounted directory provided to a podman volume
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

On host having nfs mounted directory given to container with podman run -it -v /nfsmount:/nfsmount mount will work and i can read files but running shared libraries from underlying nfs mount fails with:
error while loading shared libraries: cannot restore segment prot after reloc: Permission denied

It works fine if selinux is disabled:
setenforce 0

and selinux dumps this denial:
ausearch -m avc -ts recent

type=AVC msg=audit(1610717563.131:1724): avc:  denied  { execmod } for  pid=472453 comm="rdi_xsct" path="libgurobi65.so" dev="0:58" ino=31196134 scontext=system_u:system_r:container_t:s0:c96,c312 tcontext=system_u:object_r:nfs_t:s0 tclass=file permissive=0


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. setup a nfs mount on host:
mount -t nfs nfsserver:/nfsmount /nfsmount

2. run podman with -v /nfsmount:/nfsmount

3. try running a command which links a shared library on /nfsmount

**Describe the results you received:**
selinux blocks running of application

**Describe the results you expected:**
App should start normally.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Running on Fedora 32.

Full selinux details:
```
SELinux is preventing rdi_xsct from execmod access on the file libgurobi65.so.

*****  Plugin catchall (100. confidence) suggests   **************************

If you believe that rdi_xsct should be allowed execmod access on the libgurobi65.so file by default.
Then you should report this as a bug.
You can generate a local policy module to allow this access.
Do
allow this access for now by executing:
# ausearch -c 'rdi_xsct' --raw | audit2allow -M my-rdixsct
# semodule -X 300 -i my-rdixsct.pp

Additional Information:

Hash: rdi_xsct,container_t,nfs_t,file,execmod
```

comments.body:
Turn on the virt_use_nfs boolean and this will be allowed.

# setsebool -P virt_use_nfs 1

Thanks @rhatdan! I'm not able to test this right now as my Fedora 32 is hit by another known selinux issue:
https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1818990

Still do you think virt_use_nfs should be part of default selinux policy for Fedora Workstation containers?
No because I am not sure that many people share content with their VMs and Containers via NFS.
